column1: Draft Rate Assumptions — Avg Picks Per Round Per Position (12-Team Baseline)

These rates power the scarcity model in Tab 5. Scale = league_size / 12. R1-4: ~50% RB / ~40% WR / ~10% QB+TE. QB cascade: spike in R5-6 after first QB triggers run. TE elite spread across picks 15-35, NOT a cluster. Rows in yellow = QB cascade rounds.

column7: ~50% RB / ~40% WR — almost no QB; rare elite TE (top TE sometimes pick 12-15)
Still RB/WR heavy; 1 elite TE possible (pick 13-24 range)
TE elite tail end (pick 25-35); first early QB sometimes here
QB run begins — first QB triggers cascade; TE second wave starts
★ QB RUN PEAKS — 2nd and 3rd QB follow quickly after first
★ QB CASCADE continues — positions 2 & 3 of QB elite tier
QB run normalizes; WR depth building

column1: QB CASCADE (yellow rows): First QB triggers panic — next 2 follow within ~10 picks. In larger leagues (16+) this cascade can start in R3-4.
TE SPREAD: Top 3 TEs go across picks 15-35 (not a cluster). ~1 per round in R1-3 at most. Second wave (TE4-8) comes in R4-8. This is why TE Elite exhausts after R3-4 even with low early rates.
